pick.rationale: BYU holds a meaningful advantage in efficiency metrics, ranking 23rd-33rd nationally compared to Texas's 37th-45th range, with a roughly 4-point adjusted efficiency margin gap and significantly better defense. While Texas enters with momentum from their narrow First Four victory over NC State and BYU is missing second-leading scorer Richie Saunders to a season-ending ACL tear, the Cougars have had over a month to adjust without him and still feature elite offensive firepower led by freshman phenom AJ Dybantsa. Texas's poor defensive ranking should be exploitable by BYU's top-15 offense, and the Cougars' Big 12 pedigree and superior overall quality give them the edge in what should be a competitive 6/11 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: BYU vs Texas - 6/11 West Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: BYU favored despite Saunders injury due to efficiency advantage and elite offense, but Texas's momentum and clutch performance make this closer than typical 6/11 matchup.
reasoningStep.evidence: BYU ranks 23rd-33rd in KenPom/Torvik vs Texas 37th-45th; BYU offense 10th-16th ranked vs Texas 13th-21st; BYU defense 88th vs Texas 125th in Torvik; BYU missing star guard Richie Saunders (season-ending ACL, 2nd leading scorer); Texas won First Four 68-66 vs NC State with Tramon Mark game-winner; BYU 23-11 record vs Texas 18-14; historical 6-seed win rate approximately 63-65% vs 11-seeds.

result.text: NCAA Tournament preview: Four things to know about BYU’s opponent, Texas window.googletag = window.googletag || {cmd: []}; googletag.cmd.push(function() { googletag.defineSlot('/21740664820/SLC/KSLSPORTS/kslsports_scoreboard_logo_standard', [140, 75], 'div-gpt-ad-1731350445481-0').addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.defineSlot('/21740664820/SLC/KSLSPORTS/kslsports_scoreboard_logo_small', [90, 25], 'div-gpt-ad-1731350469980-0').addService(googletag.pubads()); googletag.enableServices(); }); Close Log In/Sign Up sports.ksl.com Home Advertise with KSL Sports Download the KSL Sports app Listen to KSL Sports Zone Subscribe to our Newsletters KSL Sports Zone Schedule Watch Contests Share With Us Meet Our Team Podcasts Cougar Tracks The Utah Blockcast Jazz Notes Utah Hockey Podcast All Podcasts Teams Olympics Utah Jazz Utah Mammoth Utah Utes BYU USU RSL Utah Royals NFL Utah Warriors High School NFL Locals Salt Lake Bees --> KSL.com/sports KSL TV KSL NewsRadio KSL Outdoors NBC Sports Discover | Log In/Sign Up College Sports Pro Sports High School Podcasts Watch Menu On the Site: College Basketball Bracket Mayhem KSL Sports Ultimate Video Game Bracket BYU BASKETBALL NCAA Tournament preview: Four things to know about BYU’s opponent, Texas Mar 18, 2026, 2:25 AM | Updated: 2:26 am BY MITCH HARPER KSL Sports PORTLAND, Ore. – BYU basketball will take on the Texas Longhorns in the First Round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Texas defeated NC State to reach the Round of 64 against AJ Dybantsa and the 6-seed BYU. Here are four things to know about 19-14 Texas. #1 First season for Sean Miller on the Forty Acres After being fired at Arizona following the 2021 season, Miller spent three seasons at Xavier, guiding the Musketeers to two NCAA Tournament appearances. That led to Miller landing with Texas, where the season has been up and down. Miller started his first year in the SEC at 0-2, then closed out the season dropping five of the last six, before the First Four win on Tuesday night in Dayton. Miller has appeared in the NCAA Tournament 13 times as a head coach, and in those appearances, he’s reached the second weekend of the Big Dance eight times. In 2007, during Miller’s first stint at Xavier, he coached against BYU in the NCAA Tournament, defeating the Cougars in a first round matchup at Rupp Arena in Lexington, Kentucky. #2 NBA prospect: Dailyn Swain The star player to watch for Texas is Xavier transfer Dailyn Swain, who followed Sean Miller to Texas this season. The 6-foot-8 wing is viewed as a potential pick in the 2026 NBA draft. Swain was selected as a Second Team All-SEC performer this season. He also earned SEC Newcomer of the Year. This season, Swain has averaged 17.8 points, 7.6 rebounds, and 3.4 assists per game. #3 Defense has been optional this season for Texas, until the NCAA First Four Texas has seven losses this season in which they have scored more than 80 points. The Longhorns are 250th nationally in scoring defense this s

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Texas defeated NC State to reach the Round of 64 against AJ Dybantsa and the 6-seed BYU. Here are four things to know about 19-14 Texas. #1 First season for Sean Miller on the Forty Acres After being fired at Arizona following the 2021 season, Miller spent three seasons at Xavier, guiding the Musketeers to two NCAA Tournament appearances. That led to Miller landing with Texas, where the season has been up and down. Miller started his first year in the SEC at 0-2, then closed out the season dropping five of the last six, before the First Four win on Tuesday night in Dayton. Miller has appeared in the NCAA Tournament 13 times as a head coach, and in those appearances, he’s reached the second weekend of the Big Dance eight times. In 2007, during Miller’s first stint at Xavier, he coached against BYU in the NCAA Tournament, defeating the Cougars in a first round matchup at Rupp Arena in Lexington, Kentucky. #2 NBA prospect: Dailyn Swain The star player to watch for Texas is Xavier transfer Dailyn Swain, who followed Sean Miller to Texas this season. The 6-foot-8 wing is viewed as a potential pick in the 2026 NBA draft. Swain was selected as a Second Team All-SEC performer this season. He also earned SEC Newcomer of the Year. This season, Swain has averaged 17.8 points, 7.6 rebounds, and 3.4 assists per game. #3 Defense has been optional this season for Texas, until the NCAA First Four Texas has seven losses this season in which they have scored more than 80 points. BYU (6-seed, 23-11):

The 4-point efficiency margin and Dybantsa's potential give BYU a slight advantage, though I'm not highly confident given the circumstances. I'll lean toward BYU but acknowledge this could easily go either way.
